title: CIRCIA Quarterly Compliance Attestation — General Counsel signs end-to-end compliance posture across statutory + NPRM clauses
exclusions: Excludes deferred-attestation periods during transition (e.g., NPRM-to-Final-Rule). Excludes pre-CIRCIA legacy compliance work. Excludes attestations on subsidiary-only entities where the parent attestation suffices.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.54.26` archived weekly to `audit_evidence` with `circia_clause="CIRCIA-s2242b"`, `circia_clause="CIRCIA-s2242c"`, `circia_clause="CIRCIA-s2242d"`, `circia_clause="CIRCIA-NPRM-72hr-reporting"`, `circia_clause="CIRCIA-NPRM-24hr-ransom"`. Every row pins (attestation_id, attestation_period_quarter, posture_72hr_sla, posture_24hr_sla, posture_supplemental_sla, posture_preservation, posture_signed_at, posture_signed_by, attestation_status). Dashboard `CIRCIA — Quarterly Compliance Attestation` shows the rolling 4-quarter attestation history.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Quarterly attestation aggregates the 72-hour SLA posture from UC-22.54.2 and UC-22.54.10 and provides a single executive-signed posture statement.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the 72-hour SLA posture is reviewed and signed by an authorised executive each quarter.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.54.26` archived to `audit_evidence` with the signed attestation document.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Aggregates the 24-hour ransom-clock posture from UC-22.54.3 and UC-22.54.11.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the 24-hour ransom-clock posture is reviewed and signed by an authorised executive each quarter.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Aggregates supplemental-report SLA posture from UC-22.54.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the supplemental-report SLA is reviewed and signed each quarter.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Skip the quarterly attestation for the current quarter; UC-22.54.26 MUST flag `attestation_status="overdue"` within 14 days of quarter-end.
controlTest.negativeScenario: General Counsel signs the attestation within 14 days of quarter-end, capturing the four posture fields. UC-22.54.26 MUST record `attestation_status="current"`.

dataSources: KV Store `circia_attestation_lookup`. Aggregate of UC-22.54.2, UC-22.54.3, UC-22.54.4, UC-22.54.5 quarterly summary metrics from `audit_evidence`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup circia_attestation_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval quarter_end_at = case(
    attestation_period_quarter="Q1", strptime(attestation_period_year . "-04-15", "%Y-%m-%d"),
    attestation_period_quarter="Q2", strptime(attestation_period_year . "-07-15", "%Y-%m-%d"),
    attestation_period_quarter="Q3", strptime(attestation_period_year . "-10-15", "%Y-%m-%d"),
    attestation_period_quarter="Q4", strptime(attestation_period_year+1 . "-01-15", "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval attestation_status = case(
    isnull(posture_signed_at) AND quarter_end_at < now_ts, "overdue",
    isnull(posture_signed_at), "pending",
    isnull(posture_signed_by), "unsigned",
    posture_signed_at < (now_ts - 365 * 86400), "stale",
    1=1, "current")
| where attestation_status IN ("overdue", "unsigned", "stale")
| table attestation_id attestation_period_quarter attestation_period_year quarter_end_at posture_72hr_sla posture_24hr_sla posture_supplemental_sla posture_preservation posture_signed_at posture_signed_by attestation_status
description: Tracks the quarterly General-Counsel attestation across the four CIRCIA-SLA postures. Surfaces overdue or unsigned attestations.
value: An executive-signed quarterly attestation gives the board, auditors, and external counsel a single source of truth on CIRCIA posture. Without it, the entity has to reconstruct posture under pressure.
implementation: (1) SOAR `circia-attestation-prefill` aggregates UC-22.54.2, .3, .4, .5 summaries into a quarterly snapshot. (2) General Counsel reviews and signs within 14 days of quarter-end. (3) Schedule UC-22.54.26 weekly Monday 06:00.
visualization: (Panel 1) Quarterly attestation history with all four postures. (Panel 2) SLA-trend chart over rolling 4 quarters. (Panel 3) Overdue queue. (Panel 4) Posture-deterioration alerts.

references.title: CIRCIA — General Counsel Attestation Best Practices

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Holiday-period delays** — Q4 attestation may legitimately slip into January-second-half due to year-end. Suppression: extend Q4 grace to Feb 15.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `circia_22_54_26_exceptions` with documented CISO approval and 30-day maximum grant.
reviewer: CIRCIA Compliance SME Panel + General Counsel
equipmentModels: Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, audit_evidence summary index)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- KV Store `circia_attestation_lookup`.
- SOAR `circia-attestation-prefill` playbook.

## Step 1 — Collection

```ini
[circia_attestation_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.attestation_id = string
field.attestation_period_quarter = string
field.attestation_period_year = number
field.posture_72hr_sla = string
field.posture_24hr_sla = string
field.posture_supplemental_sla = string
field.posture_preservation = string
field.posture_signed_at = number
field.posture_signed_by = string
```

## Step 2 — Prefill playbook

`circia-attestation-prefill` runs at quarter-end and writes a draft row with each posture field aggregated from `audit_evidence` summary metrics.

## Step 3 — Search and alert

```ini
[UC-22.54.26: CIRCIA Quarterly Compliance Attestation]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * 1
dispatch.earliest_time = -1y@y
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.54.26,reg=CIRCIA
action.notable.param.rule_title = CIRCIA quarterly attestation overdue
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Runbook for `overdue`** (owner: General Counsel, severity P3): within 14 days sign attestation.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**CIRCIA-specific failure modes**: posture decline signals leadership action; trending downward should escalate to CISO immediately.
grandmaExplanation: Every three months, the head lawyer reviews and signs a one-page report saying 'yes, our cyber-incident reporting machine is working' or 'these parts need fixing'. If they skip a quarter, the team is alerted.
